_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch — Hampton Roads (Norfolk/Virginia Beach) delegation, districts 93-97, all sitting Democrats re-elected/seated for the 2026 session. State tier. Evidence-first finalization from primary roll-call/patron records (LIS via ChoiceTracker/BillTrack/FastDemocracy), advocacy endorsements (Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA, Giffords), and news (Virginia Mercury, ACLU-VA, Ballotpedia). All five show uniformly progressive HARD records on the marquee culture votes — abortion (HJ1 reproductive-freedom amendment), same-sex marriage (HJ9/HJ3), and the 2026 assault-weapons-ban / gun-control package — scored FALSE with citations. NULL where no public record was found (no party inference). NOTE: Kelly Convirs-Fowler has FLAGGED-BUT-UNVERIFIED 2022 cross-party deviations (Freedom Index logs YES on a CRT-curriculum ban HB787, a Family Life Education parental-transparency bill HB789, and a religious-freedom-of-worship bill HB775, plus a 2020 abstention on ending police qualified immunity citing her deputy-sheriff husband). The researcher cautioned these conservative-scorecard roll-calls can read counterintuitively and recommended primary LIS confirmation; LIS blocks automated fetch, so these cells are left NULL pending a human roll-call check rather than credited on an unverified source. Documented in her notes for upgrade.

profile.religion: Non-denominational Christian (per official Virginia House biographical record)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-93 (City of Norfolk), sitting since a 2022 special election; re-elected Nov 2025 (term to Jan 2028). U.S. Navy veteran (2002-2013); small-business owner / DEI consultant. Chairs the Election Administration subcommittee of House Privileges & Elections.

jackie-glass.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-93 (Norfolk); Navy veteran; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier — overnight autopilot] Uniformly progressive HARD record on the marquee culture votes: co-patron + YES on HJ1 (2025 reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment, House 51-48; re-passed 2026 to the Nov ballot); co-patron of the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (HJ9 2025 / HJ3 2026, all-Democrat YES bloc); voted for the 2026 assault-weapons / large-capacity-magazine ban (signed into law by Gov. Spanberger). Sponsored HB1265 (2026) limiting ICE/federal-immigration cooperation in courthouses and sensitive locations — a documented sanctuary-style measure. Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia. iVoterGuide classifies her 'Verified Liberal' (Grade 10). Police-related work (HB1281/HB250 interrogation-reform) is accountability-oriented, NOT pro-police-funding advocacy → public_justice left null. No conservative-direction deviation found. Cells with no public record (most of family/child-sovereignty, christian-liberty, economic-stewardship, election-integrity specifics, federal-overreach) left null — not inferred from party.

0.note: Co-patron of and voted YES on HJ1 (2025), the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House passed 51-48); re-sponsored in 2026 to the ballot — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Supported the full reproductive-freedom amendment enshrining abortion access — opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Co-patron of the constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban (HJ9 2025 / HJ3 2026); voted with the all-Democrat YES bloc.

1.note: YES on codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Sponsored HB1265 (2026), a sanctuary-style measure restricting ICE/federal-immigration cooperation in courthouses and near schools/polling places — opposes federal preemption against sanctuary policy.

1.note: Voted for the 2026 assault-weapons / large-capacity-magazine ban (signed into law) — supports the 'assault weapons' ban this dimension opposes.

profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-94 (City of Norfolk); first elected Nov 2023, re-elected Nov 2025. Civil-rights attorney (J.D., UC Berkeley); former senior policy analyst on President Obama's White House Domestic Policy Council; education-nonprofit policy executive.

phil-hernandez.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-94 (Norfolk); civil-rights attorney, former Obama DPC analyst; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier — overnight autopilot] Uniformly progressive HARD record: voted YES on HJ1/SJR247 (reproductive-freedom amendment) and was a chief co-patron in 2023; patron of a wall of contraception/abortion-access bills (HB1716, HB2371, HB609, HB819). Patron/supporter of the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (HJ9, all-Democrat YES). Co-patron of $15 minimum wage (HB1, vetoed 2024), farm-worker wage-floor (HB866), and a new higher income-tax bracket (HB2333). Giffords-endorsed; co-patron of the assault-weapons/large-capacity-magazine ban (HB217) and patron of HB229 banning firearms in hospitals/psychiatric facilities. Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia. No conservative-direction deviation found. Religion, family/child-sovereignty, christian-liberty, public-justice, federal-overreach, and immigration cells left null — no public record located (not inferred from party or ethnicity).

0.note: Voted YES on HJ1/SJR247 (reproductive-freedom amendment) and was a chief co-patron of the amendment in 2023 — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Patron/YES across a wall of abortion-access and contraception bills (HB1716, HB2371, HB609, HB819) — opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Patron/supporter of HJ9 (2025) repealing Virginia's man-woman marriage definition; voted with the all-Democrat YES bloc (House 58-35).

1.note: YES on codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

Giffords-endorsed; co-patron of the assault-weapons / large-capacity-magazine ban (HB217) and pledged to protect Virginia's extreme-risk (red-flag) law — supports the bans/red-flag laws this dimension opposes.

profile.religion: Christian; states active membership at New Jerusalem Ministries (Hampton Roads), where he mentors youth (denomination not publicly specified)
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-95 (parts of Norfolk + Virginia Beach); served HD-85 2020-2022, lost 2021, returned 2024 (HD-95), re-elected Nov 2025. Career campaign operative / legislative chief of staff; B.A. Hampton University. Sits on House Education and Appropriations.

alex-askew.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-95 (Norfolk/Virginia Beach); legislative chief of staff by trade; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier — overnight autopilot] Uniformly progressive HARD record: co-patron of HJ1 (2025 reproductive-freedom amendment, House 51-48); part of the all-Democrat YES bloc on the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (HJ9 2025 / HJ3 2026, House 67-31). Co-sponsored HB1993 (state-agency DEI strategic-plan mandate); voted for the $15 minimum wage, paid family/medical leave, and repeal of the public-employee collective-bargaining ban. Giffords-endorsed; voted for the 2026 Democratic gun-control package incl. the assault-weapons ban. Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia. No conservative-direction deviation found. family/child-sovereignty K-12-curriculum cells, christian-liberty, public-justice, federal-overreach, and immigration left null — HB1993 is a state-agency DEI mandate, not a K-12-curriculum vote, so it was not used to score family_child_sovereignty q2; no other public record located.

0.note: Co-patron of HJ1 (2025), the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 51-48) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Supported the reproductive-freedom amendment enshrining abortion access — opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Part of the all-Democrat YES bloc on the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (HJ3 2026, House 67-31; HJ9 2025).

1.note: YES on codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

Giffords-endorsed (credited with background-check expansion and red-flag/ERPO reforms); voted for the 2026 House gun-control package incl. the assault-weapons ban — supports the bans/red-flag laws this dimension opposes.

profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-96 (Virginia Beach/Chesapeake); first Asian-American woman elected to the VA House (2017); served the 21st District 2018-2024, then HD-96; re-elected Nov 2025. Former elementary-school teacher (M.Ed., Old Dominion); married to a deputy sheriff. Often characterized as comparatively moderate.

kelly-convirs-fowler.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-96 (Virginia Beach); former teacher; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier — overnight autopilot] Progressive HARD record on the marquee votes: YES on HJ1 (2025 reproductive-freedom amendment, House 51-49); YES on the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (HJ9 2025); Giffords-endorsed and voted for the firearm-violence-intervention office (HB1736) and red-flag-law record. Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia. *** MODERATE / CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS FLAGGED FOR HUMAN VERIFICATION (left NULL, not credited): a conservative scorecard (Freedom Index) logs her voting YES in 2022 on a CRT-curriculum ban (HB787), a Family Life Education parental-transparency bill (HB789), and a religious-freedom-of-worship bill (HB775); and Wikipedia documents her 2020 ABSTENTION on ending police qualified immunity (HB5013), citing her deputy-sheriff husband. The researcher cautioned these conservative-scorecard roll-calls can read counterintuitively (procedural/amendment votes) and recommended confirming them on the primary LIS roll-call; LIS blocks automated fetch, so per evidence-first discipline these cells are NULL pending a human check rather than credited on an unverified source. If a human confirms the HB787/HB789 votes, family_child_sovereignty q2 may upgrade to TRUE; HB775 → christian_liberty q3. The qualified-immunity abstention is a recusal, not affirmative pro-police advocacy, so it does not meet the public_justice q0 bar even if confirmed. *** Other cells left null — no public record.

0.note: Voted YES on HJ1 (2025), the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 51-49) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Supported the reproductive-freedom amendment and contraception-access bills (HB609, HB2371) — opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Voted YES on the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (HJ9 2025, House 58-35; 2026 second-passage cognate).

1.note: YES on codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

Giffords-endorsed (credited with background-check expansion and helping pass Virginia's red-flag law); voted for HB1736 (firearm-violence-intervention office) — supports the red-flag/gun-control regime this dimension opposes.

profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-97 (Virginia Beach); elected Nov 2023 (defeated GOP incumbent Karen Greenhalgh), re-elected Nov 2025. U.S. Air Force veteran — 20 years, retired Master Sergeant; cybersecurity entrepreneur (M.S., Norfolk State). Member, Virginia Legislative Black Caucus. Committees: Public Safety, Transportation, Labor & Commerce.

michael-feggans.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-97 (Virginia Beach); Air Force MSgt (ret., 20 yrs), cybersecurity entrepreneur; re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier — overnight autopilot] Uniformly progressive HARD record on the marquee votes: patron + YES on HJ1 (2025 reproductive-freedom amendment, House 51-46) and featured in Planned Parenthood's 'Bros for Repro' with a direct pro-choice statement; patron of the same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (HJ9 2025 / HJ3 2026). Voted YES on the $15 minimum wage (HB1, committee); patron of offshore-wind workforce (HB67), a 'Buy American/Virginia' procurement preference (HB1361), and a 5% veteran-owned-business procurement set-aside (HB381). Giffords-endorsed; campaign supports expanded background checks, the red-flag law, and safe-storage. Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia. No conservative-direction deviation on any culture/life/2A dimension — the only mildly cross-cutting items (Buy-American + veteran-business procurement) are stewardship-flavored, not conservative-coded, and don't map to an applicable cell. family/child-sovereignty, christian-liberty, election-integrity, public-justice, federal-overreach, and immigration cells left null — no public record located.

0.note: Patron of and voted YES on HJ1 (2025), the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 51-46); PP-endorsed with a direct pro-choice statement — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Sponsored/supported the reproductive-freedom amendment enshrining abortion access — opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Patron of HJ9 (2025) repealing Virginia's constitutional same-sex-marriage ban (HJ3 2026 second passage).

1.note: YES / sponsorship on codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.
Giffords-endorsed; campaign explicitly supports expanded background checks, strengthening the extreme-risk (red-flag) law, and safe-storage mandates — supports the regime this dimension opposes.
